title: More Russian snooping allegations
summary: After Hungary was accused of leaking sensitive EU discussions to the Kremlin, the spotlight is now shifting to Germany. Zoya Sheftalovich is joined by Ian Wishart to unpack mounting concerns in Brussels over the far-right AfD’s access to confidential EU documents — and whether Europe’s open systems are creating new vulnerabilities. The duo also discuss […]

title: Intensive deployment causing Russian jets to glitch (Ukraine Battlefield update, day 1,488)
summary: Leaked documents reveal frequent Russian aircraft technical failures and crew errors, as Ukraine successfully downed a Ka-52 helicopter using FPV drones. On the ground, Russian forces advanced near Hryshyne, while Ukraine reported repelling a major mechanised assault near Borove.

title: Greece accused of deploying Afghan paramilitaries to attack asylum seekers
summary: Operating under the supervision of three Greek officers, the Afghan unit robbed, stabbed, threatened rape, and beat 61 newly-arrived migrants in 2023, says the report.

title: Crimea air-defences bleeding, drone downs Russian Ka‑52 chopper (Ukraine Battlefield update, day 1485)
summary: A Ukrainian drone knocked out a Russian helicopter in a rare incident, while massed drone attacks degraded air defences in Russian-occupied Crimea.

title: [Interview] Journalist who was followed by Russian agents in Vienna: ‘If they want to find something out, they will’
summary: Interview with Anna Thalhammer, who exposed Austrian journalists suspected of working for Moscow, why the country treats Russian espionage so casually, and how its history has shaped this attitude.

title: EU leaders enraged as summit ends with Orbán vetoing €90bn Ukraine aid
summary: EU leaders failed to convince Hungary to lift its veto on Ukraine support, as president Volodomyr Zelensky lamented the blockage.

title: Macron seeks Ramadan ceasefire, as EU leaders say no to Trump on Iran
summary: "As the [Middle East] region enters a period of religious holidays, I think everyone should calm down and the fighting should stop," said French president Macron in Brussels.

title: Spain’s Sánchez attacks other EU leaders for exploiting energy prices to gut climate policies
summary: “Unfortunately, some governments are using this crisis and the rise in electricity prices to undermine climate policies,” Spain's socialist prime minister Pedro Sanchéz said on arrival at an EU leaders summit in Brussels.

title: 24 days to election: Three Ukrainians banned from Hungary as ‘threat to Orbán’s life’
summary: With just over three weeks to go, a literal 'false flag' operation at a Tisza rally, a potential JD Vance (but not Donald Trump) visit, some suspect candidates name-changing to confuse voters, and the latest opinion polling.

title: Ukrainian ‘birds’ kill record 900 Russian soldiers, after fog-veiled attack (Ukraine Battlefield update, day 1,484):
summary: "Just before zero hour [midnight], the first [Russian] assault groups ... moved off in the drizzle and ran straight into the drones," said a Ukrainian commander.

title: President von der Leyen discusses the Middle East crisis, Ukraine and competitiveness with EU leaders
summary:
European Commission News Brussels, 23 Mar 2026 
President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, participated in the European Council meeting held in Brussels on 19 March 2026. She joined the EU l...

title: Danish premier Frederiksen poised to retain power as right splinters
summary: Also in this newsletter: How to tackle Europe’s air defence shortage

title: The secret to Ukraine’s remarkably resilient labour market
summary: Flexibility, financial support and remote work have offset some of the economic upheaval of war

summary: Ukrainian parliament at odds with president over tax and anti-corruption measures

title: EU and Australia clinch trade and security pacts
summary: Both sides are looking to reduce their dependence on the US and China

title: EU to clinch trade deal with Australia after eight years of talks
summary: Also in this newsletter: Greek shipowner questions EU sanctions on Russia amid energy price rise

title: ‘It’s always Putin’ behind every EU migration crisis, official says
summary: Russian president is ‘biggest driver’ of immigration due to his role in wars over past decade, says EU commissioner

title: Memo from Paris: controversy and gastronomy at France’s biggest farm show
summary: Despite the absence of cows this year due to disease concerns, the annual Salon International de l’Agriculture still dazzled with other livestock, as well as the finest regional flavours

title: Ukraine nationals detained in India: Is Russia involved?
summary: Indian authorities accuse six Ukrainians and an American of supporting insurgent groups along the India-Myanmar border. Ukraine suggests that the case might be politically motivated.

title: EU reacts to 'concerning' report of Hungarian leak to Russia
summary: The European Union and Germany have expressed concern at reports of Hungary's foreign minister briefing Russia on EU talks. Prime Minister Viktor Orban has launched an investigation into what he's calling wiretapping.

title: Ilya Remeslo, formerly a pro-Putin blogger, has ended up in a Russian psychiatric hospital after criticizing the president. What happened?
summary: After seemingly switching sides, a pro-government blogger in Russia who criticized President Vladimir Putin has been admitted to psychiatric care in St. Petersburg. Did he go rogue?

title: Mass Prague rally hits Babis over democracy concerns
summary: Demonstrators accused Prime Minister Andrej Babis of democratic backsliding, citing shifts in the country's foreign and domestic policies toward what the call more pro-Russia positions.

title: Drone defense in the Iran war: What can Ukraine offer?
summary: As the Iran war continues apace, Ukraine's hard-won expertise in countering drone swarms is sparking interest around the world.

title: EU summit: Hungary holds Ukraine aid ransom over Druzhba oil
summary: Hungary and Slovakia have said they will not approve a major loan package for Ukraine until oil deliveries from Russia through the Druzhba pipeline are restored. EU leaders failed to sway them in Brussels.

title: Irish metals refinery is in supply chain that feeds Russian war machine, records suggest
summary:
Shipments to Russian smelters from Aughinish Alumina have increased sharply since the invasion of Ukraine

A leading Irish metals refinery is part of an international aluminium supply chain that appears to conclude with shipments to arms producers feeding the Kremlin’s war machine in Ukraine, leaked records and public data suggests.

Trading records show that shipments to Russian smelters from Aughinish Alumina, which is located on the Shannon estuary in the west of Ireland and has been owned by the Russian aluminium group Rusal since 2006, have increased sharply since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

Continue reading...

title: Denmark election: far right has slowed under Frederiksen – but at what cost?
summary:
Polling for anti-immigration DPP is relatively low, but many feel its ideas have been co-opted by Mette Frederiksen’s Social Democrats

Mayasa Mandia, a recent graduate living in the small Danish town of Kokkedal, will be voting for the left in Tuesday’s general election – but it won’t be for Mette Frederiksen’s Social Democrats.

The 23-year-old, a practising Muslim, says that under Frederiksen’s government far-right commentary has become normalised in the Danish mainstream. She has seen this, she says, at her own university, where there were discussions about banning prayers.

Continue reading...

title: Ukraine war briefing: Russia setting up long-range drone bases in Belarus, Zelenskyy says
summary:
Ukraine president vows to respond to move that would draw Belarus more directly into the war; EU anger at Hungary over Russia information sharing. What we know on day 1,490

Continue reading...

title: European Commission president warns of ‘brutal, harsh and unforgiving’ world — video
summary:
In an address to Australia’s parliament, the European Commission president, Ursula von der Leyen, says Europe offers stability in an increasingly uncertain world, but that stability is changing. Europe has faced more than four years of conflict in Ukraine since Russian’s invasion, along with the new threat of war in the Middle East. ‘The comfort blanket of yesterday is ripped away. It is confronting,’ she says. On Tuesday, Australia and the European Union struck an agreement that will lead to both sides slashing tariffs and expanding trade across a range of areas

Continue reading...

title: Giorgia Meloni concedes defeat in Italy’s judiciary reform referendum – as it happened
summary:
Italian prime minister says she will respect the vote but says it is ‘a lost change to modernise Italy’

Meanwhile, Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orbán attempted to get on the front foot after allegations that his foreign minister Péter Szijjártó was leaking confidential EU talks to Russia as he ordered a probe into what he called a wiretapping of Szijjártó’s phone.

“We are dealing with two serious issues: there is evidence that Hungary’s foreign minister was wiretapped, and we also have indications of who may be behind it. This must be investigated immediately,” Orban tweeted on Monday, as reported by Reuters.

Continue reading...

title: Hungarian election candidate accuses ruling party of treason over alleged EU leak to Russia
summary:
Péter Magyar, who leads in polls, says Orbán government is ‘betraying Hungarian and European interests’

  • Europe live – latest updates

The candidate leading the polls in Hungary’s upcoming elections has said the alleged sharing of confidential EU information between Budapest and Moscow should be investigated as possible treason, while the European Commission has called for “clarifications” over the alleged leaks.

Péter Magyar, a conservative anti-corruption campaigner who is mounting the most serious challenge to Viktor Orbán’s 16-year-long grip on the Hungarian premiership, said the government appeared to be colluding with Russia, “thereby betraying Hungarian and European interests”.

Continue reading...

title: Mette Frederiksen heads into Danish elections as a global figure, but a divisive one domestically
summary: The leader of the Social Democrats, in power since 2019, seeks reelection on Tuesday. While her support for Ukraine and her firm stance against Donald Trump regarding Greenland have earned her recognition on the European stage, her party has been struggling in the polls.

title: EU says reports that Hungary leaked information to Russia are 'greatly concerning'
summary: European security officials reportedly said that the Hungarian foreign minister called his Russian counterpart during breaks in EU meetings to give 'direct reports on what was discussed.'

title: Russia-linked tanker seized by France arrives in Marseille
summary: The Deyna, a 250-meter-long vessel that set sail from Murmansk in Russia under a Mozambican flag, is under EU sanctions and is the third such tanker intercepted by France in recent months.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to apply provisionally from May 1
summary: The mammoth deal to eliminate tariffs on more than 90% of trade between the two blocs has proven divisive in Europe, with France leading opposition over concerns some of its farmers will be worse off because of it.

title: Electricity prices: 'The Iberian solution has significantly reduced consumer bills without public spending'
summary: Because soaring electricity prices across Europe stem from a flaw in the market, with gas still setting the price for all, the European Union should address the root cause and take inspiration from the mechanism in Spain and Portugal, economist Natalia Fabra argues.

title: 'The oil shock in the Middle East is a godsend for Russia'
summary: By joining Israel in its war against Iran, Donald Trump is handing Vladimir Putin an unexpected economic and strategic reprieve, explains Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

title: 'Why was the threat of Iranian drones overlooked for so long?'
summary: Despite Ukraine's repeated pleas for help against Iranian drones since 2022, the country has had to develop its own interception capabilities, which are now highly sought after by the Gulf States, writes historian Jean-Pierre Filiu in his column.

title: Russia resumes use of space launch site damaged in accident
summary: Part of the launch site in Kazakhstan collapsed during the lift-off of Soyuz MS-28 in November last year, temporarily preventing Russia from being able to send cosmonauts into space.

title: Dnipro, the 'heart of Ukraine' where residents, refugees and military live side by side
summary: This industrial city, considered a stronghold of the Jewish community, embodies the country's history and a certain idea of resistance to the advances of the Russian army, four years after the start of the war.

title: Portrait of a City after Four Years of War: The Courage of Kyiv
summary: The attacks come at night. But once the sun comes up, daily life continues. After four years of war, the Ukrainian capital of Kyiv remains defiant. Where does the city's strength come from?

title: Nord Stream: How Early Did the CIA Know about the Pipeline Attack?
summary: In 2022, a Ukrainian commando blew up the Nord Stream natural gas pipeline. DER SPIEGEL has learned that the CIA may have spoken to the saboteurs during the planning stage.

title: Former U.S. Security Adviser John Bolton: "We Have Passed Peak Trump"
summary: John Bolton, Donald Trump's former national security adviser, warns Ukraine against making too many concessions in the peace negotiations. And says that Trump is being manipulated by Putin.
